MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAUL D. FIFE OF WILD CAREY & FIFE, TEL. NO. 837-3101 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ADOBE WEST LANDSCAPING COMPANY;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ADOBE WEST LANDSCAPING COMPANY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is Granted, for good cause shown. Defendant has established mistake pursuant to CCP 473(b). The former president of the dissolved corporation mistakenly thought there could be no liability because the corporation was dissolved and had no assets. A reasonable and honest mistake of the law is grounds for granting relief under CCP 473(b). (Security Truck Line v. City of Monterey (1953) 117 Cal.App.2d 441, 445-46.) Defendant's motion to set aside default is timely. Plaintiff will not be prejudiced by setting aside the default. Defendant has filed a proposed answer, no depositions have been taken yet, and defendant is not seeking to postpone the trial date which is currently set in July 2014. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
